description: Legal record (#82) in a court register/pinqas from a Karaite beit din in Cairo (on f. 28v of Yevr.-Arab. I 328). Dated 29 Av 5325 AM (1565 CE). Dispute following the death of Estīta bt. Farjallah al-Kohen aka al-Shuqayr, who had been betrothed to a certain ʿAbd al-Raḥmān b. Khiḍr al-Fūlādī. The deceased woman's father appears before the court in order to demand the return of the dowry he had given ʿAbd al-Raḥmān while his daughter was still alive, consisting of gold ashrafī jadīd coinage and a ring weighing two mithqāl. ʿAbd al-Raḥmān then states that he used the money to purchase "items for the aforementioned [Estīta]" (אלפלוס אלדי אעטיהם לי אשתרית בהם חואיג ללמד׳). ʿAbd al-Raḥmān proceeds to turn over these items and, based on their valuation, the presiding judge determines that the fiancé of the deceased woman still owes 3 ashrafī jadīd, which he commits to repay within three months. Interestingly, Farjallah allows ʿAbd al-Raḥmān to keep the gold ring he had provided as part of his daughter's dowry. MCD.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בהספרייה הלאומית רוסיה.
